title: Azure Logic Apps - Limits, Runtime, And Networking Considerations
applies_to: Network and firewall design for Logic Apps workflows

confirmed_facts.fact: Azure Logic Apps publishes separate limits and configuration guidance for multitenant Consumption logic apps and single-tenant Standard logic apps.
For multitenant Azure Logic Apps behind strict firewalls, the environment must allow the inbound and outbound IP addresses required by Azure Logic Apps in the logic app's region.
Microsoft recommends using Azure service tags instead of specific IP addresses for firewall rules, including LogicAppsManagement, LogicApps, and AzureConnectors.
For Standard logic app workflows in single-tenant Azure Logic Apps, networking restrictions must also account for connector connections and fully qualified domain names in addition to service tags.

The Logic Apps documentation includes separate operational guidance for recurrence schedules, deployment automation, unit testing from workflow definitions or runs, and DevOps setup for Standard workflows.

implementation_notes: Treat the limits document as mandatory source material before designing high-volume or network-restricted Logic Apps solutions.
For multitenant workflows, regional IP and service-tag allowances are a first-class infrastructure prerequisite, especially for HTTP triggers, webhooks, and connector callbacks.
For Standard workflows, include FQDN and connection-specific network requirements in the design, not just service tags.

When building knowledge or implementation guides, capture whether the workflow is latency-sensitive, long-running, webhook-driven, or recurrence-driven because those characteristics determine which limits matter most.
caveats: The limits page is extensive and changes over time; any production design should re-check the current limits before implementation.
Firewall design differs materially between Standard and Consumption.
Power Automate and Logic Apps share some platform infrastructure behavior, but their operational limits are not interchangeable.
